title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in keyboard-first content-safe feed reader and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.
Prefer safe text, use a tested allowlist sanitizer only where needed, rewrite links, block active content, and preserve readable structure.
Use one page heading, feed and entry landmarks, descriptive link text, metadata lists, dates, language, and source provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Untrusted Content
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 4908 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.

Inspect the Feed Go Untrusted Content trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat titles, authors, summaries, html, images, categories, and urls as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries. For Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Untrusted Content, keep Feed Go Untrusted Content connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Untrusted Content, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

For inspect the failure record, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Reflow Motion Media
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-reflow-motion-media-1t4cf5" that uses case 5332 to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Reflow Motion Media.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feed Go Reflow Motion Media into observable performance evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Reflow Motion Media, keep Feed Go Reflow Motion Media connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Responsive CSS alone proves the reader is accessible.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Support narrow reflow, zoom, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, content warnings, and user-controlled media.

options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Feed Go Reflow Motion Media.
Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the feed go reflow motion media · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For feed go reflow motion media · fault isolation, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Reflow Motion Media
steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Go Reflow Motion Media Feed aggregation and Go source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Reflow Motion Media.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Feed Go Reflow Motion Media assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Reflow Motion Media, keep Feed Go Reflow Motion Media connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 8562 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Support narrow reflow, zoom, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, content warnings, and user-controlled media.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Feed Go Reflow Motion Media
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Feed Go Reflow Motion Media: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go team handles case 1181 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Support narrow reflow, zoom, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, content warnings, and user-controlled media.
